Class Dungeon

java.lang.Object
Dungeon_Classes.Dungeon
public class Dungeon extends Object
Represents a multi-floor dungeon layout within the game world. Manages the progressive tracking of floors, verification of completion criteria, floor transition increments, and end-of-game logic when a player character is defeated.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    Dungeon(String name, int dungeonNum, int numFloors, Floor[] floors, NPChar member)
    Constructs a dungeon environment with predefined floors, resetting the current floor tracking index to its baseline starting stage.

  • Constructor Details

    • Dungeon

      public Dungeon(String name, int dungeonNum, int numFloors, Floor[] floors, NPChar member)
      Constructs a dungeon environment with predefined floors, resetting the current floor tracking index to its baseline starting stage.
      Parameters:
      name - the structural name of the dungeon
      dungeonNum - the identifying number of the dungeon
      numFloors - the ceiling number of floors inside this structure
      floors - the composite array of Floor items making up the dungeon layout

  • Method Details

    • getName

      public String getName()
      Retrieves the name string representation of the dungeon.
      Returns:
      the name string representation

    • setName

      public void setName(String name)
      Updates the identity text name of the dungeon.
      Parameters:
      name - the updated identity text name

    • getDungeonNum

      public int getDungeonNum()
      Retrieves the sequential dungeon number index.
      Returns:
      the sequential dungeon number index

    • setDungeonNum

      public void setDungeonNum(int dungeonNum)
      Updates the identification index number of the dungeon.
      Parameters:
      dungeonNum - the new identification index number

    • getNumFloors

      public int getNumFloors()
      Retrieves the total capacity boundary count of floors.
      Returns:
      the total capacity boundary count of floors

    • setNumFloors

      public void setNumFloors(int numFloors)
      Updates the upper count of contained floors.
      Parameters:
      numFloors - the new upper count of contained floors

    • getCurFloor

      public int getCurFloor()
      Retrieves the active floor location index step.
      Returns:
      the active floor location index step

    • setCurFloor

      public void setCurFloor(int curFloor)
      Updates the target step position index to track.
      Parameters:
      curFloor - the target step position index to track

    • getFloors

      public Floor[] getFloors()
      Retrieves the array holding floor records.
      Returns:
      the array holding floor records

    • setFloors

      public void setFloors(Floor[] floors)
      Updates the replacement array structure of floor nodes.
      Parameters:
      floors - the replacement array structure of floor nodes

    • getMember

      public NPChar getMember()
      Retrieves the associated member.
      Returns:
      NPChar instance

    • assignFloors

      public void assignFloors(Floor[] catalogue)
      Assigns a series of floor structures matching a specific map index catalogue.
      Parameters:
      catalogue - the input array mapping available layout references

    • isCompleted

      public boolean isCompleted(PlayableChar entity)
      Evaluates dungeon completion by checking the termination status of the final floor within the array stack.
      Parameters:
      entity - the playable character instance to evaluate
      Returns:
      true if the final map floor conditions are cleared, false otherwise

    • incrementCurFloor

      public void incrementCurFloor()
      Advances the tracking index for the player's active floor map level, capped at the defined upper bound number of maximum floors.

    • gameOver

      public boolean gameOver(PlayableChar entity)
      Monitors the user's survival state. If death is detected, it logs formatted console readouts indicating the asset culprit responsible.
      Parameters:
      entity - the user-controlled character instance to monitor
      Returns:
      true if the user character has fallen in combat or hazards, false otherwise
